title: Hedin equations and the GW closure
summary: Hedin's coupled equations organize G, W, Sigma, P, and Gamma; the GW closure is obtained by setting the vertex Gamma to unity.
assumptions: The coupled Hedin system is the right formal skeleton for the targeted quasiparticle problem.

approximations: vertex truncation Gamma approximately equals 1 for the GW closure
boundary_conditions: single-particle Green's function framework is retained
exclusions: claims that Gamma approximately equals 1 is always quantitatively accurate

applies_to: explaining where GW comes from inside many-body perturbation theory
out_of_scope: full error bounds for the GW truncation

promotion.rationale: Manual L2 seed for reusable GW/QSGW excitation memory.

payload.statement: The GW approximation is the vertex-truncated closure of Hedin's equations with Gamma approximately equal to one.
payload.proof_sketch: Start from the coupled equations for G, W, Sigma, P, and Gamma, then drop vertex corrections to obtain Sigma approximately equals iGW and P approximately equals -iGG.
